title: Military expert flags 'concrete proof' Scottish airport helps US bomb Iran

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A MILITARY intelligence expert has flagged “concrete proof” that a Scottish airport has been used to help the US bomb Iran ...

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Military expert flags 'concrete proof' Scottish airport helps US bomb Iran Glasgow Prestwick Airport is one of the airports used for military stopovers (Andrew Milligan/PA)

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. A MILITARY intelligence expert has flagged “concrete proof” that a Scottish airport has been used to help the US bomb Iran.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Military expert flags 'concrete proof' Scottish airport is helping US bomb Iran
